raw_input: Write a Python function `merge_intervals(intervals: list[list[int]]) -> list[list[int]]` that takes a list of closed intervals `[start, end]` with `start <= end`, and returns a new list of merged intervals sorted by start. Two intervals should be merged if they overlap OR touch (i.e. [1,4] and [4,5] become [1,5]). Do not mutate the input.
success_criteria: fully-contained intervals disappear into their container

inline.tests:
assert merge_intervals([]) == []
assert merge_intervals([[1,4]]) == [[1,4]]
assert merge_intervals([[1,3],[2,6],[8,10],[15,18]]) == [[1,6],[8,10],[15,18]]
assert merge_intervals([[1,4],[4,5]]) == [[1,5]]
assert merge_intervals([[1,10],[2,3],[4,5]]) == [[1,10]]
assert merge_intervals([[5,6],[1,2]]) == [[1,2],[5,6]]
assert merge_intervals([[1,4],[0,4]]) == [[0,4]]
assert merge_intervals([[1,2],[3,4],[5,6]]) == [[1,2],[3,4],[5,6]]
inp = [[1,3],[2,6]]
out = merge_intervals(inp)
assert out == [[1,6]]
assert inp == [[1,3],[2,6]], 'input must not be mutated'
